completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/integration/test_mode_c_smoke_e2e.py enqueues Mode C builds and asserts the three terminal shapes
completion_promises.evidence: Created tests/integration/test_mode_c_smoke_e2e.py with three top-level test classes (TestEmptyInitialReviewTerminal, TestFixTasksNoCommitsTerminal, TestFixTasksWithCommitsReachesPRReview) each driving a Mode C build through the supervisor and asserting the terminal shape. Harness uses real ModeCCyclePlanner + ModeCTerminalHandler against in-memory dispatcher fakes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Empty review scenario: /task-review returns zero fix tasks; no /task-work stage entry recorded; build reaches complete terminal state with clean-review rationale; no PR URL recorded
completion_promises.evidence: TestEmptyInitialReviewTerminal contains three tests: test_empty_review_reaches_clean_review_terminal asserts TurnOutcome.TERMINAL with 'clean-review' in rationale; test_empty_review_does_not_dispatch_task_work asserts only one /task-review dispatch and zero /task-work dispatches; test_empty_review_records_no_pr_url asserts pr_review_gate.submissions == [] and zero recorded pull_request_url values on chronology rows.

completion_promises.criterion_text: N fix tasks, no commits scenario: three fix tasks dispatched and approved; worktree commit count is zero; build reaches complete terminal state with clean-review rationale; no PR URL recorded
completion_promises.evidence: TestFixTasksNoCommitsTerminal scripts review_outcomes=[ReviewOutcome(('FIX-1','FIX-2','FIX-3')), ReviewOutcome(())] with commit_count=0; asserts three /task-work dispatches with sorted ids matching FIX-1/2/3, TurnOutcome.TERMINAL, 'clean-review' rationale, and pr_review_gate.submissions == [].

completion_promises.criterion_text: N fix tasks, commits exist scenario: three fix tasks dispatched and approved; worktree has at least one commit; build pauses at pull-request-review with MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL
completion_promises.evidence: TestFixTasksWithCommitsReachesPRReview scripts the same review pattern with commit_count=2 (and 4 in the second test); asserts TurnOutcome.DISPATCHED with chosen_stage=PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW, exactly one PR submission with auto_approve=False, gate_mode=='MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL', and pull_request_url starting with 'https://github.com/example/forge/pull/'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: /task-review entry precedes every /task-work entry it produced (Group G ordering)
completion_promises.evidence: TestStageHistoryShape::test_task_review_precedes_every_task_work_it_produced walks pipeline.stage_log.rows and asserts the first row is TASK_REVIEW, then iterates and asserts every TASK_WORK row has last_review_idx_seen != None and < its own index.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each /task-work entry references exactly one fix task identifier (Group B canary)
completion_promises.evidence: TestStageHistoryShape::test_each_task_work_references_exactly_one_fix_task_id asserts each work row has non-None fix_task_id and details['fix_task_id'] mirrors the row-level field. PlannedSubprocessDispatcher also raises AssertionError if TASK_WORK is dispatched without a FixTaskRef, providing belt-and-braces enforcement.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Per-fix-task artefact paths attribute only to the fix task that produced them (Group G no double-attribution)
completion_promises.evidence: TestStageHistoryShape::test_artefact_paths_attribute_only_to_producing_fix_task scripts unique artefact_paths per fix task (FIX-1 -> [src/a.py, tests/a_test.py], FIX-2 -> [src/b.py], FIX-3 -> [docs/c.md]) and walks every work row asserting no path appears under two different fix_task_ids; also positively asserts attributed_per_fix_task matches the scripted lists.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each /task-work entry carries originating_review_entry_id pointing to the /task-review entry that produced its fix task (Group L lineage)
completion_promises.evidence: TestStageHistoryShape::test_each_task_work_carries_originating_review_entry_id captures the entry_id of the first /task-review row and asserts every /task-work row's details['originating_review_entry_id'] equals that id. The harness uses build_task_work_attribution() from forge.pipeline.terminal_handlers.mode_c to populate the lineage field via the production helper.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Failure-isolation assertion (ASSUM-008): one fix task's /task-work returns failed; sibling fix tasks still get dispatched; the failed fix task's failure is recorded against itself
completion_promises.evidence: TestFailureIsolation::test_failed_work_does_not_block_sibling_dispatch scripts FIX-A=approved, FIX-B=failed, FIX-C=approved; asserts all three fix-task ids dispatched (sorted dispatched_ids == ['FIX-A','FIX-B','FIX-C']), per-id status map exactly matches scripted statuses (failure recorded only on FIX-B), and the cycle still terminates cleanly with TurnOutcome.TERMINAL.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Hard-stop assertion: /task-review returns a hard-stop result; no /task-work dispatch is recorded; build reaches failed terminal state (Group C)
completion_promises.evidence: TestHardStopReview has two tests: test_hard_stop_review_reaches_failed_terminal asserts TurnOutcome.TERMINAL with rationale containing 'failed' or 'hard-stop'; test_hard_stop_review_does_not_dispatch_any_task_work asserts zero TASK_WORK dispatcher calls and zero PR submissions when ReviewOutcome carries hard_stop=True.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cycle-termination assertion (ASSUM-010): after N fix tasks complete, a follow-up /task-review returns no further fix tasks; no further /task-work dispatch; cycle terminates with the appropriate clean or PR terminal
completion_promises.evidence: TestCycleTermination::test_followup_clean_review_terminates_after_fix_tasks_complete scripts review_outcomes with two reviews (first emitting FIX-X, FIX-Y; second clean) and asserts exactly two /task-review dispatches, two review rows, and the follow-up review is the last row in the stage log (no further /task-work dispatched). Reaches TurnOutcome.TERMINAL with no commits.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tests run in under 60 seconds with all dispatchers stubbed
completion_promises.evidence: TestPerformanceBudget::test_three_terminal_paths_complete_under_one_second runs all three terminal paths sequentially and asserts elapsed < 1.0 second (orders of magnitude under the 60-second budget). Actual full-suite run reports '17 passed in 0.06s'. All dispatchers are in-memory stubs (PlannedSubprocessDispatcher, FakePRReviewGate, FakeCommitProbe); specialist + autobuild dispatchers raise AssertionError if invoked.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Verified `python3 -m ruff check tests/integration/test_mode_c_smoke_e2e.py` reports 'All checks passed!' and `python3 -m black --target-version py312 tests/integration/test_mode_c_smoke_e2e.py` produced no further changes after the initial format pass.
